title: PSP Forced Door with Concurrent ESP Interactive Logon

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-006-6 R3 Part 3.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.27: PSP Forced Door with Concurrent ESP Interactive Logon.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.27' running on index=physical forced events, index=wineventlog RDP logons, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
spl:
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access event_type="forced" earliest=-6h
| eval ptime=_time, site=bes_site
| join type=left site [
    search index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 LogonType=10 earliest=-6h
    | lookup bes_site_map.csv ComputerName OUTPUT bes_site as site
    | stats count by Account_Name, ComputerName, site
  ]
| where isnotnull(Account_Name)
| table ptime, site, employee_id, Account_Name, ComputerName, count
description: We time-align severe physical alarms with interactive logons at the same site to support combined cyber-physical incident analysis.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes physical, wineventlog and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security, pacs:access. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain `bes_site_map.csv` from facility records. (2) Tune time bucketing (example uses join on site). (3) Prefer summary indexing for scale. (4) Attach results to CIP-008 preliminary review. Indexes required: `index=physical`, `index=wineventlog`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `sourcetype=pacs:access`.
visualization: Timeline (overlay), Table (correlated pairs), Single value (pair count)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count

grandmaExplanation: We watch for we time-align severe physical alarms with interactive logons at the same site to support combined cyber-physical incident analysis. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=physical` forced events, `index=wineventlog` RDP logons.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `bes_site_map.csv` from facility records. (2) Tune time bucketing (example uses join on site). (3) Prefer summary indexing for scale. (4) Attach results to CIP-008 preliminary review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access event_type="forced" earliest=-6h
| eval ptime=_time, site=bes_site
| join type=left site [
    search index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 LogonType=10 earliest=-6h
    | lookup bes_site_map.csv ComputerName OUTPUT bes_site as site
    | stats count by Account_Name, ComputerName, site
  ]
| where isnotnull(Account_Name)
| table ptime, site, employee_id, Account_Name, ComputerName, count
```

Understanding this SPL

**PSP Forced Door with Concurrent ESP Interactive Logon (CIP-006-6 R3 Part 3.2)** — We time-align severe physical alarms with interactive logons at the same site to support combined cyber-physical incident analysis.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=physical` forced events, `index=wineventlog` RDP logons. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: pacs:access. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype=pacs:access, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ptime** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(Account_Name)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**PSP Forced Door with Concurrent ESP Interactive Logon (CIP-006-6 R3 Part 3.2)** — We time-align severe physical alarms with interactive logons at the same site to support combined cyber-physical incident analysis.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=physical` forced events, `index=wineventlog` RDP logons. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: Account_Name, ComputerName, count, employee_id, ptime, site.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (overlay), Table (correlated pairs), Single value (pair count)
